name: Satellogic Inc.
business: Satellogic is a vertically integrated Earth observation company that designs, manufactures, and operates satellite systems, delivering decision-grade insights at scale to government and commercial customers. Its strategy is focused on two business lines: Data & Analytics (including tasking, archived data, Aleph Observer, and Constellation as a Service) and Space Systems (selling satellites and related services).
namedCustomers: Centre of Engineering and Product Development in Portugal (CEiiA)

exposures.sentence: Satellogic is a vertically integrated Earth observation company that designs, manufactures, and operates satellite systems, delivering decision-grade insights at scale to government and commercial customers.
Our 2025 NextGen platform introduces 30cm-class resolution and onboard edge computing, allowing for real-time AI processing directly on the satellite to deliver insights rather than just raw pixels.

This integrated approach enables Satellogic to deploy satellites on predictable timelines and operate with capacity to support persistent coverage across large portfolios of sites. Satellogic enables continuous monitoring and alert-driven workflows that help defense and intelligence ( D&I ) agencies, civil governments, and commercial operators move from reactive tasking to proactive decision-making, providing mission-critical data when it is needed.

Our Space Systems business offers unique solutions to sovereign customers or local partners with their own EO capabilities and in-orbit flight heritage at a disruptive price by leveraging our vertical integration and non-ITAR design.

catalysts.event: First satellite launch of Merlin constellation
catalysts.sentence: Merlin, our AI-First constellation, is expected to launch its first satellite in the fourth quarter of 2026, and be fully operational in the first half of 2027.
Merlin, our AI-First constellation, is expected to launch its first satellite in the fourth quarter of 2026, and be fully operational in the first half of 2027.
NextGen is expected to be operational in 2028.

On February 23, 2026, the Company announced the launch of Aleph Observer, its persistent monitoring capability designed to deliver sustained, large-scale situational awareness across critical locations worldwide.

On December 31, 2025, the Company entered into an $18 million agreement with CEiiA, the Centre of Engineering and Product Development in Portugal, for the supply and in-orbit delivery of two NewSat Mark V satellites... Ownership and operational control of the satellites are expected to transfer to CEiiA in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Revenue start from $30 million defense contract
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, we were awarded a multi-year contract valued at $30 million with a strategic international defense and security customer... with revenue on the contract expected in 2027.
On January 29, 2026, the Company entered into an 11-month contract extension with the Government of Albania to continue providing country-wide, high-frequency satellite monitoring services.

tamClaims.claim: EO sector projected to contribute $3.8 trillion to global GDP between 2023 and 2030
tamClaims.sentence: According to a recent analysis by the World Economic Forum, the EO sector is projected to contribute a cumulative $3.8 trillion to global gross domestic product (GDP) between 2023 and 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Cumulative global manufacturing revenue for Earth Observation satellites will be approximately $31 billion in 2026 and $101 billion by 2034
tamClaims.sentence: Recent market research estimates that the cumulative global manufacturing revenue for Earth Observation satellites will be approximately $31 billion in 2026 and will reach approximately $101 billion by 2034.
